Fix toolbar About button not appearing

text label. "About" was only passed as the tooltip so the button
rendered with no icon and no text. Pass it for both args.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: communication tool executors (#524)

## Summary

- Replace stub executors for `sessions_ask` and `message` with real
implementations
- Add new `sessions_send` tool (fire-and-forget cross-agent messaging)
- Introduce `ToolServices` pattern: `CrossNousService` and
`MessageService` traits in organon, with adapter implementations in the
main binary
- Thread `ToolServices` through `NousManager` -> `NousActor` ->
`ToolContext`

## Details

**New types** (`crates/organon/src/types.rs`):
- `CrossNousService` trait: `send()` and `ask()` for cross-agent
communication
- `MessageService` trait: `send_message()` for outbound Signal messaging
- `ToolServices` struct: optional service locator on `ToolContext`

**Executors** (`crates/organon/src/builtins/communication.rs`):
- `MessageExecutor`: validates 4000-char limit, delegates to
`MessageService`
- `SessionsAskExecutor`: delegates to `CrossNousService.ask()` with
configurable timeout
- `SessionsSendExecutor`: delegates to `CrossNousService.send()` with
configurable session key

**Adapters** (`crates/aletheia/src/main.rs`):
- `CrossNousAdapter`: wraps `CrossNousRouter`, constructs
`CrossNousMessage`
- `SignalAdapter`: wraps `ChannelProvider`, uses agora's target parsing
(phone/group)

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo test -p aletheia-organon` (49 tests pass, including 14 new
communication tests)
- [x] `cargo test -p aletheia-nous` (186 tests pass)
- [x] `cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings` (zero
errors)
- [ ] Manual: verify `sessions_send` dispatches to registered agent
- [ ] Manual: verify `message` sends via Signal when provider configured

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(cli): remove hardcoded REPO_ROOT that breaks npm installs (#147)

REPO_ROOT computed the monorepo root by navigating up from CLI_ROOT,
which points to the wrong location when the CLI is installed as an
npm global package. Since REPO_ROOT is not actually used by any
command, remove it entirely along with stale test mocks.

Closes #86

update, find-by-id, and list-active-workers. WorkerService wraps DAO
with transaction management and serialization.

Phase C2.0 of distributed job worker.

commit.message:
feat(entities): populate fecCommitteeId via FEC API verification

Ran scripts/verify-entities.mjs against the FEC committees API.
Results: 51 verified, 398 unverified, 0 skipped.

Verified (51 entities with FEC committee IDs now populated):
  walmart, meta, spacex, lowes, exxonmobil, koch-industries,
  jpmorgan-chase, bank-of-america, tyson-foods, altria,
  lockheed-martin, best-buy, dollar-tree, publix, macys, gap-inc,
  sherwin-williams, autozone, autonation, patagonia, tractor-supply,
  petsmart, conocophillips, phillips-66, citigroup, stripe,
  dell-technologies, mgm-resorts, caesars-entertainment,
  las-vegas-sands, paramount, unitedhealth-group, elevance-health,
  humana, mckesson, cencora, cardinal-health, pfizer, mondelez,
  general-mills, campbell-soup, conagra, smithfield-foods,
  yum-brands, darden-restaurants, bridgestone-usa, wyndham,
  royal-caribbean, delta-air-lines, outback-steakhouse, gopuff.

Unverified entries are written to scripts/unverified-entities.txt.
The 429 rate-limit errors account for most unverified entries —
re-running the script will populate those on the next API window.
The unverified list also includes private/foreign companies with
no FEC committee presence (ALDI, IKEA, Shein, LVMH subsidiaries, etc.).

commit.message:
Release v3.5.0: Type Synthesis, Quantum Spectral Sequence, Monodromy (Amendments 28-30)

Amendment 28 — ψ-Pipeline Inversion (Type Synthesis):
- type: TypeSynthesisGoal, TypeSynthesisResult, SynthesizedType, MinimalConstraintBasis
- resolver: TypeSynthesisResolver, ConstraintSearchState
- derivation: SynthesisStep; observable: SynthesisSignature
- 7 TS_ identities + proofs; SHACL test40, test41

Amendment 29 — Quantum Level Spectral Sequence:
- type: QuantumLift, LiftObstruction
- observable: SpectralSequencePage, LiftObstructionClass
- resolver: IncrementalCompletenessResolver, LiftRefinementSuggestion
- 6 QLS_ identities + proofs; SHACL test42, test43

Amendment 30 — Monodromy Observables:
- observable: HolonomyGroup, DihedralElement, Monodromy, MonodromyClass, ClosedConstraintPath
- type: FlatType, TwistedType; resolver: MonodromyResolver
- 7 MN_ identities + proofs; SHACL test44, test45

Codegen: add collect_inherited_assoc_types() to prevent E0221 ambiguous
associated type errors when subtrait parent already declares the same assoc type.

Counts: 175 classes, 325 properties, 637 individuals; 109 conformance checks.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: batch status fetches to prevent agentic-tools pod restarts

When includeStatus=true, the resource list endpoint was firing N concurrent
kubectl calls simultaneously via Promise.all, overwhelming the agentic-tools
pod (500m CPU) and causing liveness probe failures. Now processes status
fetches in batches of 5.

commit.message:
Fix aggregation queries failing with Socrata 400 errors

fetchDataset was injecting the default $order (e.g. session_start_dt DESC)
into every query, including aggregation queries using SUM/COUNT/AVG/MAX in
$select or $group. Socrata returns 400 when ordering by a field not in the
SELECT of an aggregation query.

Now skips the default sort when the query contains $group or aggregate
functions in $select. Callers can still explicitly pass $order to override.

Fixes: stat cards, trend indicators, and data freshness not appearing on
Parking Revenue (and potentially other views with aggregation queries).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
